w:r.w:t: 2. Hybrid Intelligence Architecture — How It Actually Works
8. AI Explainability Layer — Claude API Integration
9. Data Sources — APIs and How to Use Them
14. Demo Script — How to Present to Judges
NexusAgent is an autonomous AI risk intelligence layer that continuously monitors DeFi wallets,
detects threats before funds are lost, and executes protective actions automatically.
Every year, billions of dollars are lost in DeFi due to:
Rug pulls — developers abandon project and drain liquidity
Protocol exploits — smart contract vulnerabilities get hacked
Liquidation cascades — collateral drops below threshold, funds auto-liquidated
Malicious contract approvals — users unknowingly give unlimited spending permissions
Stablecoin depegs — USDC/USDT loses peg, portfolio value crashes
Existing tools give raw data. They don't protect users. NexusAgent does.
Scores wallet 0-100 based on 12 risk signals
Analyzes any token contract for red flags
Tracks TVL, liquidity anomalies in real-time
Explains every risk decision in plain language
Shows AI is grounded, not fake
Simulates market crash, shows wallet response
NOT: 'AI crypto assistant' → sounds like vaporware
NOT: 'AI trading bot' → sounds like gambling
This single framing change moves you from 'interesting student project'
Pure LLM (Claude only): Judges see through it in 30 seconds. 'LLM wrapper.'
Pure rule engine: No AI, just if-else logic. Not impressive.
Hybrid = Rule engine calculates WHAT the risk is. Claude explains WHY.
This is the architecture that looks real, is real, and wins.
This layer does REAL calculations. No AI. No guessing. Hard math.
// Signal 3: Mint function exists and is unrestricted
This produces a NUMBER. Deterministic. Explainable. Real.
Claude receives the calculated risk data and explains it in human language.
Claude does NOT calculate risk. Claude only explains what the engine found.
You are a DeFi security analyst. A risk engine has calculated these signals
- Liquidity lock: 14 days (HIGH RISK — too short)
- Owner holds: 48% of supply (HIGH RISK — concentrated)
- Mint function: ACTIVE with no restrictions (HIGH RISK)
Write a 3-sentence explanation a non-technical user can understand.
Be direct. Mention the most critical risk first.
Do NOT recommend any specific action.`,
// 'This token shows a high rug pull probability of 78/100.
// The primary concern is that the team holds 48% of all tokens
// and the liquidity is only locked for 14 days, meaning they could
// drain the pool and disappear at any time.
// The active mint function also allows unlimited token creation,
// which would immediately dilute your holdings.'
File-based routing, server components, faster than Create React App
Fastest way to build professional dark UI without writing CSS from scratch
Smooth animations for dashboard score changes — judges notice polish
Clean charts for TVL and risk history graphs
You already know JS — don't switch to Python just for backend
Latest stable with built-in overflow protection
Best testing framework, easy deployment scripts
Pre-audited contracts — use their Ownable, ReentrancyGuard
Standard library for blockchain interaction in JS
React hooks for wallet — easiest wallet integration
Beautiful wallet connect UI in 10 lines of code
ONLY for explanation generation — not for risk calculation
TVL data, protocol metrics — best free DeFi data source
Token holder data, contract source code, transactions
Token prices, market cap, price history
Free testnet, fast transactions, zero cost for demo
One-click Next.js deployment — perfect for hackathon demo
Deploy Node.js backend — easier than AWS for hackathon
Create this exact folder structure on Day 1. This is your project map.
Keep contracts SIMPLE. You are not building a new DeFi protocol.
1. NexusDefender.sol — stores user risk settings and triggers
That is ALL. Complex Solidity = bugs = lost marks.
5.1 Setup Hardhat Project — Step by Step
Open terminal. Go to your nexusagent folder.
Run these commands one by one:
# Step 1: Create contracts folder and enter it
# Step 6: Install dotenv for environment variables
# Should say: Compiled 0 Solidity files successfully
This is your main contract. It stores each user's risk settings and defense preferences.
uint8 riskTolerance; // 1-10, how aggressive defense should be
// Events — blockchain log of all actions
event ProfileCreated(address indexed user, uint8 riskTolerance);
event ProfileUpdated(address indexed user, uint8 riskTolerance);
event DefenseTrigger(address indexed user, string action, uint256 timestamp);
// Log a defense action (called by backend when action happens)
function logDefenseAction(address user, string calldata action)
This contract revokes dangerous ERC-20 approvals. This is your killer feature on-chain.
// Standard ERC-20 interface — just the approve function
function approve(address spender, uint256 amount) external returns (bool);
function allowance(address owner, address spender) external view returns (uint256);
// Revoke approval for ONE token from ONE spender
// User calls this directly — they sign the transaction
function revokeApproval(address tokenAddress, address spender) external {
// Revoke approvals for MULTIPLE tokens in one transaction
This is the HEART of NexusAgent. This is what makes AI feel real. Build this carefully.
// 12 risk signals — each contributes to final score
const totalValue = tokens.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.valueUSD, 0);
const finalScore = signals.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s.score, 0);
detail: 'Owner can create unlimited tokens', weight: 20 });
detail: 'Team can change contract logic after deployment', weight: 15 });
const top10Percent = holders.slice(0, 10).reduce((s, h) => s + h.percent, 0);
detail: `Top 10 wallets hold ${top10Percent}% of supply`, weight: 15 });
# Step 1: Create and enter backend folder
npm install express cors dotenv axios ethers
app.listen(PORT, () => console.log(`NexusAgent backend running on port ${PORT}`));
// Returns: wallet health score + AI explanation
// Step 1: Calculate risk with rule engine
// Returns: rug pull analysis + AI explanation
Claude is ONLY called AFTER the rule engine has done the calculation.
Claude receives structured data. Claude explains it. That is ALL.
Never ask Claude to calculate risk. Never ask Claude to recommend trades.
Claude = explainability layer only. This is what separates you from LLM wrappers.
You are a DeFi security analyst assistant for NexusAgent.
A deterministic risk engine has already calculated all risk scores.
Your ONLY job is to explain what the numbers mean in plain English.
- No financial advice (never say 'you should' or 'invest')
- Use simple words — assume user is not technical
- Always mention the most critical risk first
- Be direct and factual, not alarmist
Write 3 sentences explaining what this means for the wallet owner.`;
Write 3 sentences explaining the risk signals found.`;
// If Claude fails, return a basic explanation — app still works
9. Data Sources — APIs and How to Use Them
Token prices, market cap, price history
// Get all ERC-20 token balances for a wallet
// Parse and return unique token list
// Get contract ABI (to check for mint, proxy, etc.)
// Get TVL for a specific protocol
// Get all DeFi protocols TVL list
# Would you like to use TypeScript? NO (keep it JS for speed)
# Would you like to use ESLint? YES
# Would you like to use Tailwind CSS? YES
# Would you like to use src/ directory? YES
# Would you like to use App Router? YES
# Would you like to customize the import alias? NO
{/* Big score card — takes full width */}
export default function WalletHealthScore({ data, loading }) {
<span className={`inline-block mt-2 px-4 py-1 rounded-full border text-sm font-medium ${colorClass}`}>
<div className='max-w-md bg-gray-800 rounded-xl p-4 border border-gray-700'>
11. Attack Simulation Mode — The Demo Killer Feature
Judges can interact with simulations directly.
They remember interactive demos 10x more than static dashboards.
When a judge clicks 'Simulate Market Crash' and sees the dashboard react —
that is the moment they decide you deserve top marks.
Build this in Week 7. It takes 1 day but adds massive demo value.
description: 'Top held token liquidity drained in 60 seconds',
'CRITICAL: Abnormal liquidity outflow detected on TOKEN_X',
Set up GitHub repo, folder structure, README
Set up backend (Node.js + Express), health endpoint
Register for all API keys (Etherscan, CoinGecko)
Write Etherscan API wrapper + test with real wallet
Can fetch token list for any address
Write DefiLlama wrapper + test protocol TVL fetch
Can get TVL for Uniswap, Aave
Returns rug score for any token
Connect Claude API for rug pull explanation
Wire up risk routes — all endpoints returning data
Test all endpoints with Postman or curl
Deploy contracts to Polygon Mumbai testnet
Get free testnet MATIC from faucet, test on-chain
Integrate on-chain calls into backend routes
Write simulate routes — all 3 scenarios returning
Full backend integration test — all endpoints
Set up Next.js, Tailwind dark theme, wallet connect
User can scan any token address
Build ProtocolMonitor — TVL chart with Recharts
Build AlertFeed — real-time looking alerts list
Connect all components to backend APIs
UI polish — spacing, colors, hover states, loading
This is fake money — use it freely for testing
If faucet is slow, try: https://mumbaifaucet.com
14. Demo Script — How to Present to Judges
0:00 – 0:30 Open with the problem (1 shocking statistic)
0:30 – 1:00 Connect wallet live, show health score load
1:00 – 1:30 Scan a token address — show rug pull detection
1:30 – 2:00 Click 'Simulate Market Crash' — show dashboard react
2:00 – 2:30 Show AI explanation — explain it is grounded, not hallucinated
2:30 – 3:00 One sentence on architecture — close with impact statement
'In 2024, over 2 billion dollars were lost to DeFi exploits, rug pulls, and
liquidation cascades. Not because users were careless — but because no tool
gave them intelligent, real-time protection. NexusAgent is that tool.'
14.2 When Judge Asks 'Why Use AI Here?'
'DeFi ecosystems generate thousands of on-chain signals every minute —
liquidity changes, whale movements, contract interactions, price anomalies.
No human can monitor all of this continuously.
Our hybrid system uses deterministic rules to calculate what the risk is,
and AI to explain why it matters in language any user understands.
The AI never guesses — it only interprets what the engine already calculated.'
Sending raw data to Claude and displaying response as 'AI'
Judges see through it in 30 seconds — 'LLM wrapper'
Always calculate first with rule engine, then explain with Claude
Building complex DeFi protocols in Solidity
Bugs, security holes, wasted time — judges don't reward complexity
Keep 3 simple contracts. Complexity is in the intelligence layer
If judge inspects network tab they see no real API calls
Use real APIs even if data is testnet — authenticity matters
Judges judge what they see. Bad UI = bad project
Spend 40% of time on frontend polish and animations
Not deploying live — showing localhost only
Judges cannot verify it works after you leave the call
Deploy frontend to Vercel, backend to Railway before demo
Overcomplicating the architecture in the pitch
Judges do not want to understand your code in 3 minutes
Say: 'Rule engine calculates risk, AI explains it' — that is enough
30% → Product positioning (security infrastructure, not crypto app)
Most teams get this backwards. Do not be most teams.
Build smart. Build clean. Demo confidently. Win.
